info.name: EasyRisparmio API
info.description:
API collection for the EasyRisparmio Italian energy comparison & switching platform.

## Modules
- **Authentication** — Email/password + social login (Google, Facebook, Apple via Firebase)
- **Referrals** — Invite & Earn referral program for users and admin management
- **Meters** — Utility meter management (electricity, gas, water, internet) with admin moderation
- **Suppliers** — Energy supplier management with public listing and admin CRUD

## Roles
| Role | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `personal` | Individual user (default) |
| `business` | Business user with company profile |
| `admin` | Platform administrator (seeded, cannot register) |

## Token Flow
1. Login or social-login returns `accessToken` (JWT, 15 min) + `refreshToken` (UUID, 7 days)
2. Use `accessToken` in `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header for protected endpoints
3. When expired, call refresh-token to get a new pair

## Setup
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to your server (default: `http://localhost:3000`)
2. Register a user, then login — tokens are auto-saved to collection variables
3. All protected requests automatically use the saved `accessToken`

variable.description: Base URL of the EasyRisparmio API server
JWT access token (auto-set by login/social-login requests)
UUID refresh token (auto-set by login/social-login requests)
Referral UUID (auto-set by Create Invite request)
Meter UUID (auto-set by Create Meter request)
Supplier UUID (auto-set by Create Supplier request)

item.name: Authentication
item.description: Email/password + social login (Google, Facebook, Apple via Firebase). JWT access tokens (15 min) + UUID refresh tokens (7 days). OTP verification and password reset flows.

item.name: Registration
item.description: User registration endpoints. After registration, the account status is `pending_verification` until the email OTP is verified. Admin role cannot be used. An optional `referralCode` can be provided to link the new user to a referrer.

request.description:
Creates a personal user account.

After registration, a 6-digit OTP is generated for email verification. The account status is `pending_verification` until the OTP is verified via `POST /auth/verify-otp`.

**Validation Rules:**
- `email` — must be a valid email, unique across all users
- `password` — minimum 8 characters
- `firstName` / `lastName` — required, max 100 characters
- `phone` — optional, max 20 characters
- `role` — must be `personal` or `business` (admin is blocked)
- `referralCode` — optional, 8-char code from an existing user. If valid, the referral is linked automatically.

response.name: 201 - Registration Successful
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "message": "Registration successful. Please verify your email.",
    "user": {
      "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
      "email": "mario.rossi@email.com",
      "firstName": "Mario",
      "lastName": "Rossi",
      "phone": "+393331234567",
      "role": "personal",
      "status": "pending_verification",
      "authProvider": "local",
      "firebaseUid": null,
      "emailVerified": false,
      "phoneVerified": false,
      "createdAt": "2026-06-09T10:00:00.000Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-06-09T10:00:00.000Z"
    }
  }
}

response.name: 400 - Validation Error

request.description:
Creates a business user account with a company profile.

Extends personal registration with Italian business fields.

**Additional Fields:**
- `companyName` — required, max 255 characters
- `partitaIva` — required, exactly 11 digits (Italian VAT number)
- `pecEmail` — optional, valid email (certified PEC email)
- `legalRepresentative` — optional, max 255 characters
- `companyType` — optional (e.g. S.r.l., S.p.A.)
- `atecoCode` — optional, max 10 characters (economic activity code)
- `referralCode` — optional, links the new user to a referrer

response.name: 201 - Business Registration Successful
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "message": "Registration successful. Please verify your email.",
    "user": {
      "id": "b2c3d4e5-f6a7-8901-bcde-f12345678901",
      "email": "info@rossi-srl.it",
      "firstName": "Mario",
      "lastName": "Rossi",
      "phone": "+393331234567",
      "role": "business",
      "status": "pending_verification",
      "authProvider": "local",
      "firebaseUid": null,
      "emailVerified": false,
      "phoneVerified": false,
      "createdAt": "2026-06-09T10:00:00.000Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-06-09T10:00:00.000Z"
    }
  }
}

response.name: 400 - Invalid Partita IVA

item.name: Login
item.description: Authentication endpoints for email/password and social login. Both return a JWT access token and UUID refresh token on success.

request.description:
Authenticates with email and password. Returns JWT access token (15 min) and UUID refresh token (7 days).

**Login is blocked when:**
- Invalid credentials
- Account `pending_verification` or `suspended`
- Social-only user (no password set)

**Auto-saves** `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.

response.name: 401 - Email Not Verified

request.description:
Login or register via Google, Facebook, or Apple using Firebase Authentication.

**New user** — account created with `role: personal`, `status: active`
**Existing email** — Firebase UID linked to existing account

**Auto-saves** tokens to collection variables.

response.name: 400 - No Email on Social Account

item.name: OTP & Password
item.description: OTP verification and password reset flows.

request.description:
Verifies a 6-digit OTP code.

**Types:** `email_verification` (activates account), `phone_verification`, `password_reset`

**Behavior:** 10-minute expiry, single-use, previous unused OTPs of same type are invalidated.
Requests a password reset OTP. Always returns the same response regardless of whether the email exists (prevents user enumeration).

response.name: 200 - Reset OTP Sent

request.description: Resets password using OTP from forgot-password. After success, all refresh tokens are revoked.
Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new token pair. Old token is revoked (rotation). Auto-saves new tokens.
Returns the authenticated user's profile. Requires JWT access token. `passwordHash` is never included.

item.name: Referrals
item.description: Invite & Earn referral program. Users share referral codes to invite friends and earn EUR 10 per qualified referral. Admin manages the referral lifecycle: PENDING -> REGISTERED -> QUALIFIED -> REWARDED.

item.name: User
item.description:
User-facing referral endpoints for the Invite & Earn program. Available to `personal` and `business` users.

**Flow:**
1. Get your referral code via `GET /referrals/my-code`
2. Share the link or send targeted invites via `POST /referrals/invite`
3. Track your referrals via `GET /referrals/my-referrals`
4. Earn €10 per qualified referral when admin approves

request.description:
Returns the user's unique 8-character referral code, a shareable registration link, and aggregate referral stats.

If the user doesn't have a code yet, one is auto-generated on the first call.

**Stats include:**
- `totalInvites` — total referrals created
- `registered` — referred users who signed up
- `qualified` — referrals that met qualification criteria
- `rewarded` — referrals where reward was credited
- `totalEarnings` — total EUR earned from referrals

Returns a paginated list of referrals created by the authenticated user. Each referral shows status, referred email/phone, and referred user details if they registered.

**Query Parameters:**
- `page` — page number (default: 1)
- `limit` — items per page (default: 20, max: 100)
- `status` — optional filter: `pending`, `registered`, `qualified`, `rewarded`, `expired`

response.name: 200 - My Referrals List
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "data": [
      {
        "id": "r1a2b3c4-d5e6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",

        "referralCode": "AB3KX7WN",
        "referredEmail": "friend@email.com",

        "status": "registered",
        "rewardAmount": null,
        "rewardCreditedAt": null,
        "expiresAt": "2026-09-07T12:00:00.000Z",

        }
      },
      {
        "id": "r2b3c4d5-e6f7-8901-bcde-f23456789012",

        "referralCode": "AB3KX7WN",

        "referredUser": null
      }
    ],
    "meta": {
      "total": 2,
      "page": 1,
      "limit": 20,
      "totalPages": 1
    }
  }
}

request.description:
Creates a new referral invite with `pending` status.

Both fields are optional:
- **With email/phone** — targeted invite for a specific person
- **Without** — generic invite (anyone with the code can use it)

Invites expire after **90 days**. The user's referral code is auto-generated if they don't have one yet.

**Auto-saves** `referralId` to collection variables for use in admin endpoints.

response.name: 201 - Invite Created (Generic)
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "r2b3c4d5-e6f7-8901-bcde-f23456789012",
    "referrerId": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
    "referralCode": "AB3KX7WN",
    "referredEmail": null,
    "referredPhone": null,
    "referredUserId": null,
    "status": "pending",
    "rewardAmount": null,
    "rewardCreditedAt": null,
    "expiresAt": "2026-09-07T12:00:00.000Z",
    "createdAt": "2026-06-09T12:00:00.000Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-06-09T12:00:00.000Z"
  }
}

item.name: Admin
item.description:
Admin endpoints for managing the referral program. Requires `admin` role.

**Status lifecycle:** PENDING → REGISTERED → QUALIFIED → REWARDED (any → EXPIRED)

**KPIs** include total counts by status and total rewards paid in EUR.

request.description:
Returns aggregate KPIs for the entire referral program: total referrals, breakdown by status, and total rewards paid in EUR.

Requires **admin** role.

Returns a paginated list of all referrals with referrer and referred user details.

**Filters (all optional):**
- `status` — `pending`, `registered`, `qualified`, `rewarded`, `expired`
- `referrerId` — UUID of the referrer user
- `dateFrom` / `dateTo` — ISO 8601 date range on created_at
- `search` — matches referrer name, email, or referral code

Requires **admin** role.

Returns a single referral with full referrer and referred user details.

Requires **admin** role. The `referralId` variable is auto-set by the Create Invite request.

Updates a referral's status to `qualified`.

**Valid transitions:**
- `pending` → `registered` or `expired`
- `registered` → `qualified` or `expired`
- `qualified` → `rewarded` or `expired`
- `rewarded` and `expired` are terminal

Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Invalid Status Transition

request.description:
Updates a referral's status to `rewarded` and credits the reward amount.

**`rewardAmount`** (EUR) is required when setting status to `rewarded`. The `rewardCreditedAt` timestamp is set automatically.

Only valid from `qualified` status. Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Missing rewardAmount
400 - Invalid Transition (not from qualified)

request.description:
Marks a referral as expired. Can be done from `pending`, `registered`, or `qualified` status.

`rewarded` and `expired` are terminal states and cannot be transitioned from.

Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Cannot Expire Terminal State

item.name: Meters
item.description: Utility meter management (electricity POD, gas PDR, water, internet). Admin creates and moderates meters via status (active/inactive/pending/terminated). Users only see their active meters.

item.name: User
item.description:
User-facing meter/utility endpoints. Returns only `active` meters for the authenticated user.

Users typically have 2-5 utility meters (electricity, gas, water, internet). No pagination needed.

request.description:
Returns the authenticated user's active meters with supplier and address details.

Only meters with `active` status are returned. Meters in `pending`, `inactive`, or `terminated` status are hidden from the user.

No pagination — users typically have 2-5 utilities.

response.name: 200 - Active Meters List

item.name: Admin
item.description:
Admin endpoints for managing utility meters. Requires `admin` role.

**Utility types:** `electricity` (POD), `gas` (PDR), `water`, `internet`

**Status lifecycle (moderation):**
- `pending` → `active` or `terminated`
- `active` → `inactive` or `terminated`
- `inactive` → `active` or `terminated`
- `terminated` = permanent (no further transitions)

Only `active` meters are visible to users. Admin uses status to moderate visibility.

request.description:
Creates a new meter/utility point for a user.

**Required fields:**
- `userId` — UUID of the meter owner
- `utilityType` — `electricity`, `gas`, `water`, or `internet`
- `meterCode` — POD/PDR identifier (max 50 chars)

**Optional fields:**
- `supplierId`, `status` (defaults to `active`), `annualConsumption`, `consumptionUnit`, `contractedPowerKw`, `addressId`, `activationDate`, `notes`

The `createdBy`/`updatedBy` audit fields are auto-set from the admin JWT.

**Duplicate prevention:** The combination of `meterCode` + `utilityType` must be unique (returns 409 if duplicate).

**Auto-saves** `meterId` to collection variables.

response.name: 400 - Validation Error
409 - Duplicate Meter Code

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all meters with user, supplier, and address relations.

**Filters (all optional):**
- `utilityType` — `electricity`, `gas`, `water`, `internet`
- `status` — `active`, `inactive`, `pending`, `terminated`
- `userId` — filter by meter owner
- `supplierId` — filter by supplier
- `search` — matches meter code, user name, or email

Requires **admin** role.

Returns a single meter with full user, supplier, and address details.

Requires **admin** role. The `meterId` variable is auto-set by the Create Meter request.

response.name: 200 - Meter Detail
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "m1a2b3c4-d5e6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
    "userId": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
    "utilityType": "electricity",
    "meterCode": "IT001E556779",
    "supplierId": "b2c3d4e5-f6a7-8901-bcde-f12345678901",
    "status": "active",
    "annualConsumption": "12500.00",
    "consumptionUnit": "kWh",
    "contractedPowerKw": "3.00",
    "addressId": "c3d4e5f6-a7b8-9012-cdef-123456789012",
    "activationDate": "2025-01-15",
    "notes": "Verified via supplier portal",
    "createdBy": "admin-uuid",
    "updatedBy": "admin-uuid",
    "createdAt": "2026-06-01T10:00:00.000Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-06-09T12:00:00.000Z",
    "user": {
      "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
      "email": "mario.rossi@email.com",
      "firstName": "Mario",
      "lastName": "Rossi"
    },
    "supplier": {
      "id": "b2c3d4e5-f6a7-8901-bcde-f12345678901",
      "name": "Ener Energia"
    },

      "streetAddress": "Via Roma 42",
      "city": "Milano",

    }
  }
}

request.description:
Updates meter fields. All fields are optional.

For status changes, use the dedicated `PATCH /meters/:id/status` endpoint instead.

The `updatedBy` audit field is auto-set from the admin JWT.

Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 409 - Duplicate Meter Code After Update

request.description:
Marks a meter as deleted by setting `deleted_at`. The meter is hidden from all queries but preserved in the database for audit purposes.

Requires **admin** role.

Activates a meter, making it visible to the user.

Valid from `pending` or `inactive` status.

Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Invalid Transition

request.description:
Deactivates a meter, hiding it from the user.

Valid from `active` status only. The meter can be reactivated later.

Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Invalid Transition

request.description:
Permanently terminates a meter. This is a **terminal state** — no further status transitions are allowed.

Valid from `pending`, `active`, or `inactive` status.

Requires **admin** role.

response.name: 400 - Already Terminated

item.name: Suppliers
item.description: Energy supplier management. Public users browse active suppliers. Admins manage the full lifecycle including creation, updates, activation/deactivation, and soft-deletion.

item.name: Public
item.description: Public supplier endpoints. No authentication required. Returns only active suppliers.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of active suppliers. No authentication required.

Supports text search by supplier name and supplier code.

**Query parameters:**
- `page` — Page number (default: 1)
- `limit` — Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)
- `search` — Search by supplier name or supplier code

response.name: 200 - Active Suppliers List
response.body:
{

}

request.description:
Returns a single supplier with its associated offers. No authentication required.

**Path parameters:**
- `id` — Supplier UUID

item.name: Admin
item.description:
Admin endpoints for managing energy suppliers. Requires `admin` role.

Admin can create, update, soft-delete, and toggle active/inactive status of suppliers. Inactive suppliers are hidden from the public listing but remain visible to admins.

request.description:
Creates a new energy supplier.

**Required fields:**
- `name` — Supplier name (max 255 chars)

**Optional fields:**
- `logoUrl` — Logo URL (max 500)
- `description` — Supplier description
- `rating` — 0 to 5 with up to 2 decimal places (default: 0)
- `contactEmail` — Valid email (max 255)
- `contactPhone` — Phone number (max 20)
- `website` — Valid URL (max 500)
- `supplierCode` — Unique supplier code (max 50, returns 409 if duplicate)

The `createdBy`/`updatedBy` audit fields are auto-set from the admin JWT.

**Auto-saves** `supplierId` to collection variables.

response.name: 201 - Supplier Created
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "s1a2b3c4-d5e6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
    "name": "Enel Energia",
    "logoUrl": "https://cdn.easyresparmio.it/logos/enel-energia.png",
    "description": "Leading Italian energy supplier since 1962",
    "rating": "4.50",
    "isActive": true,
    "contactEmail": "info@enelenergia.it",
    "contactPhone": "+39023456789",
    "website": "https://www.enelenergia.it",
    "supplierCode": "ENEL-001",
    "createdBy": "admin-uuid",
    "updatedBy": "admin-uuid",
    "createdAt": "2026-06-10T12:00:00.000Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-06-10T12:00:00.000Z"
  }
}

response.name: 400 - Validation Error
409 - Duplicate Supplier Code

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of all suppliers (active and inactive). Requires admin role.

**Query parameters:**
- `page` — Page number (default: 1)
- `limit` — Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)
- `isActive` — Filter by active status (`true` or `false`)
- `search` — Search by name, email, or supplier code

response.name: 200 - All Suppliers (Admin)
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "data": [
      {
        "id": "s1a2b3c4-d5e6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
        "name": "Enel Energia",
        "logoUrl": "https://cdn.easyresparmio.it/logos/enel-energia.png",
        "description": "Leading Italian energy supplier since 1962",
        "rating": "4.50",
        "isActive": true,
        "contactEmail": "info@enelenergia.it",
        "contactPhone": "+39023456789",

request.description:
Updates supplier fields. All fields are optional (partial update).

Use `PATCH /:id/toggle-status` for active/inactive toggling.

The `updatedBy` audit field is auto-set from the admin JWT.

response.name: 200 - Supplier Updated

{
  "success": true,

response.name: 409 - Duplicate Supplier Code

request.description:
Soft-deletes a supplier by setting `deleted_at` timestamp. The supplier is hidden from all queries but preserved in the database.

This is a permanent action — unlike toggling `isActive`, soft-deleted suppliers cannot be recovered via the API.

Activates a supplier, making it visible in the public listing.

Active suppliers appear in `GET /suppliers`. Inactive suppliers are hidden from public but still visible to admins via `GET /suppliers/admin`.

response.name: 200 - Supplier Activated
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "s1a2b3c4-d5e6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",

    "createdAt": "2026-06-01T10:00:00.000Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-06-10T14:00:00.000Z"
  }
}

request.description:
Deactivates a supplier, hiding it from the public listing.

Inactive suppliers are still visible to admins via `GET /suppliers/admin` and can be reactivated at any time.
